info.description:
An API for manipulating Grist sites, workspaces, and documents.

# Authentication
<SecurityDefinitions />
info.title: Grist API

Soft-delete the workspace by moving it to trash. The workspace can be
restored using the unremove endpoint. If the `permanent` query parameter
is set to true, the workspace is permanently deleted instead.

parameters.description: If true, permanently delete instead of moving to trash

get.description:
Get all available templates. Templates are organized by workspace within
a template organization configured on the server.

**Note:** Requires a template organization to be configured on the server.

Get information about a specific template.

**Note:** Requires a template organization to be configured on the server.

Get all widget definitions from the configured widget repository.
Custom widgets extend Grist's functionality with specialized views.

post.description:
A unified endpoint for creating documents. Can create an empty document,
copy an existing document, or import a file.

- To create an empty unsaved document: provide no parameters
- To create an empty saved document: provide `workspaceId`
- To copy an existing document: provide `sourceDocumentId`, `workspaceId`, and `documentName`
- To import a file: use multipart/form-data with a file upload and optional `workspaceId`

workspaceId.description: The workspace to save the document in. If omitted, creates an unsaved document.

documentName.description: Name for the new document (required when copying)

sourceDocumentId.description: ID of document to copy from. Requires workspaceId and documentName.

asTemplate.description: When copying, if true, copy structure only without data

workspaceId.description: The workspace to save the document in

schema.description: a unique identifier for the document

get.description: Describe a document

patch.summary: Modify document metadata (but not its contents)
patch.description: Modify document metadata (but not its contents)

post.description:
Soft-delete the document by moving it to trash. The document can be
restored using the unremove endpoint. If the `permanent` query parameter
is set to true, the document is permanently deleted instead.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: If true, permanently delete instead of moving to trash

200.description: Document moved to trash (or permanently deleted)

post.description:
Recover a document that was previously soft-deleted. Only works if the
document is still in the trash.

200.description: Document moved successfully. Returns empty body.

patch.description:
Pin the document so it appears in a prominent location. Pinned documents
are displayed at the top of workspace listings for easy access.
Remove the pinned status from a document.

post.description:
Disabled documents cannot be accessed or modified. Moving and
renaming a disabled doc is also forbidden, as well as
accessing or submitting published forms associated to a
disabled document.

Disabled documents, however, can be moved to and restored from
the trash.

The operation is non-destructive. Disabled documents can be
re-enabled.

Only admin accounts can disable a document.

200.description: Document disabled successfully. Returns empty body.

403.description: The caller is not allowed to disable this document

post.description:
If a document has been previously disabled, this will restore
all former access to a document, including access to its
associated published forms.

Only admin accounts can enable a document.

200.description: Document enabled successfully. Returns empty body.

403.description: The caller is not allowed to enable this document

post.summary: Copies a document to a workspace.

asTemplate.description: Remove all data and history but keep the structure to use the document as a template

schema.description: a unique identifier for the document

post.description: Copies a document to a workspace.

Create a fork of a document. A fork is a personal copy that tracks its relationship
to the original document. Forks can be used to experiment with changes before
applying them to the original.

The fork will have a new docId and urlId that encode the relationship to the trunk
(original) document.

forkId.description: The unique identifier for this fork

docId.description: The full document ID of the fork

urlId.description: The URL-friendly ID of the fork

400.description: Cannot fork a document that's already a fork

403.description: Insufficient access to document to copy it entirely

get.description:
Get users that can be used with the "View As" feature for testing access rules.
Only document owners can call this endpoint.

Users are drawn from:
- Users the document is shared with
- Users mentioned in user attribute tables
- Predefined example users

get.description:
Returns a list of snapshots (backups) of the document. Snapshots are created
automatically as the document is edited. Most recent snapshots are listed first.

parameters.name: raw
parameters.description: If true, returns all snapshots including those not in the snapshot inventory

docId.description: Document ID that can be used to open this snapshot

post.description:
Remove specific snapshots from a document's backup history.
Only document owners can remove snapshots.

snapshotIds.description: List of snapshot IDs to remove
The snapshot IDs that were removed

get.description:
Returns a list of document states representing the action history.
Each state has a sequential number (n) and a hash (h) that uniquely
identifies that point in history. Most recent state is first.

Compare two versions of the same document by their state hashes.
Returns details about what changed between the versions.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Hash of the left version to compare (defaults to HEAD)

parameters.name: right
parameters.description: Hash of the right version to compare (defaults to HEAD)

parameters.name: maxRows
parameters.description: Maximum number of row changes to include in details

get.description:
Compare this document with another document. Useful for comparing
a fork with its trunk document.

parameters.name: docId2
parameters.description: ID of the document to compare with

parameters.name: detail
parameters.description: If true, include detailed change information

parameters.name: maxRows
parameters.description: Maximum number of row changes to include

keep.description: The number of the latest history actions to keep

post.description: Truncate the document's action history

get.description:
List proposals associated with a document. Proposals are suggested changes
from forks that can be reviewed and applied to the trunk document.

parameters.name: outgoing
parameters.description: If true, list proposals where this document is the source. Otherwise list proposals where this document is the destination.

retracted.description: Whether the proposal has been retracted

post.description:
Create a proposal from a fork to its trunk document. The proposal contains
the comparison of changes between the fork and trunk.
This endpoint can only be called on a fork document.

retracted.description: Set to true to retract an existing proposal

400.description: Can only propose from a fork

post.description:
Apply a proposal's changes to the document. This merges the fork's
changes into the trunk document.

parameters.name: proposalId
parameters.description: ID of the proposal to apply

post.description: Closes and reopens the document, forcing the python engine to restart.

Ensure all pending changes to the document are written to persistent storage.
Returns true if the document was flushed, false if the document was not open.

schema.description: True if document was flushed, false if document was not open

post.description:
Administrative endpoint that checks if a document is assigned to the expected
worker group and frees it for reassignment if not. Used for load balancing
and maintenance operations.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Update the document's worker group (requires special permit)

schema.description: True if document was freed for reassignment

post.description:
Replace the current document content with content from another source.
Can restore from a snapshot or copy from another document.

sourceDocId.description: ID of document to copy content from

snapshotId.description: ID of snapshot to restore from

resetTutorialMetadata.description: Reset tutorial progress when replacing (for tutorial forks)

post.summary: Set recovery mode for a document
post.description: Controls the recovery mode of a document. Recovery mode helps in recovering from errors or corrupted states. Only document owners can control recovery mode.

recoveryMode.description: Whether to enable recovery mode. Defaults to true if not specified.
The current recovery mode state of the document

403.description: Access denied - only owners can control recovery mode

get.description:
Check if formula timing is enabled for a document and retrieve timing data
if available. Only document owners can access timing information.

status.description:
- disabled: timing is not enabled
- pending: timing is enabled but no data yet
- active: timing is enabled and data is available

timing.description: Timing data (only present when status is 'active')

post.description:
Start collecting timing information for formula calculations.
Only document owners can start timing.

400.description: Timing already started for this document

post.description:
Stop collecting timing information and return the collected data.
Only document owners can stop timing.

schema.description: Collected timing data for formulas

400.description: Timing not started for this document

post.summary: Apply a list of user actions
post.description:
Apply a sequence of user actions to a document. This is a low-level endpoint
for making batch changes using Grist's internal action format.

Each action is an array where the first element is the action type.
Common action types:
- `["AddRecord", tableId, rowId, {column: value}]` - Add a row (use null for auto-assigned rowId)
- `["UpdateRecord", tableId, rowId, {column: value}]` - Update a row
- `["RemoveRecord", tableId, rowId]` - Delete a row
- `["BulkAddRecord", tableId, [rowIds], {column: [values]}]` - Add multiple rows
- `["BulkUpdateRecord", tableId, [rowIds], {column: [values]}]` - Update multiple rows
- `["BulkRemoveRecord", tableId, [rowIds]]` - Delete multiple rows
- `["AddColumn", tableId, colId, {type, ...}]` - Add a column
- `["RemoveColumn", tableId, colId]` - Remove a column
- `["RenameColumn", tableId, oldColId, newColId]` - Rename a column
- `["AddTable", tableId, [{id, type, ...}]]` - Add a table
- `["RemoveTable", tableId]` - Remove a table

parameters.name: noparse
parameters.description: If true, string values are stored as-is without parsing (e.g. dates won't be auto-converted)

requestBody.description: An array of user actions to apply

retValues.description: Return values from each action (e.g. new row IDs)

get.description: Deprecated in favor of `records` endpoints. We have no immediate plans to remove these endpoints, but consider `records` a better starting point for new projects.

post.description: Deprecated in favor of `records` endpoints. We have no immediate plans to remove these endpoints, but consider `records` a better starting point for new projects.

the IDs of rows to remove

added.description: Total files added to external storage.

200.description: The ID of the store the document's store

post.description: Start transferring attachments

get.description: Get attachment transfer status

post.summary: Delete unused attachments from the document
post.description:
When an uploaded attachment is no longer used in a Grist document,  it's retained for a period of time in case it's needed again (e.g. to facilitate an "undo").
This removes all of these retained attachments, reducing the amount of storage used. This is particularly useful if a document has hit its attachment storage limit.

schema.description: Only remove attachments that haven't been used for a period of time. This duration is set by host of the Grist instance.

post.description:
Recalculate which attachments are in use by scanning the document.
This is mostly used for testing and maintenance.

Verify that attachment records match the actual stored files.
This is a maintenance endpoint to check for data consistency.
Only document owners can call this endpoint.

get.description: Fetch records from a table

post.description: Add records to a table

requestBody.description: the records to change, with ids

put.summary: Add or update records of a table

schema.description:
Which records to update if multiple records are found to match `require`.
  * `first` - the first matching record (default)
  * `none` - do not update anything
  * `all` - update all matches
Set to true to prohibit adding records.
Set to true to prohibit updating records.
Set to true to allow `require` in the body to be empty, which will match and update all records in the table.

requestBody.description: The records to add or update. Instead of an id, a `require` object is provided, with the same structure as `fields`. If no query parameter options are set, then the operation is as follows. First, we check if a record exists matching the values specified for columns in `require`. If so, we update it by setting the values specified for columns in `fields`. If not, we create a new record with a combination of the values in `require` and `fields`, with `fields` taking priority if the same column is specified in both. The query parameters allow for variations on this behavior.

put.description: Add or update records of a table

requestBody.description: the IDs of records to remove

post.description: Delete records of a table

get.description: List tables in a document

post.description: Add tables to a document

patch.description:
Modify metadata for existing tables. This endpoint is primarily useful for:
- Renaming tables (via the `tableId` field)
- Setting on-demand loading (via the `onDemand` field)

requestBody.description: the tables to modify, identified by their current id

get.description: List columns in a table

post.description: Add columns to a table

requestBody.description: the columns to change, with ids

put.summary: Add or update columns of a table

schema.description: Set to true to prohibit adding columns.
Set to true to prohibit updating columns.
Set to true to remove existing columns (except the hidden ones) that are not specified in the request body.

requestBody.description:
The columns to add or update. We check whether the specified column ID exists: if so, the column is updated with the provided data, otherwise a new column is created.
Also note that some query parameters alter this behavior.

put.description: Add or update columns of a table

delete.summary: Delete a column of a table
delete.description: Delete a column of a table

get.description:
Get the form configuration for a specific view section. Forms are
a special view type that allows external users to submit data.

formFieldsById.description: Form fields indexed by field ID

formLayoutSpec.description: JSON layout specification for the form

formTableId.description: ID of the table the form submits to

get.description:
Returns all webhooks configured for this document, including their
settings and delivery statistics.

post.summary: Create new webhooks for a document
post.description:
Creates one or more webhooks that will POST to specified URLs when
data in the document changes. Returns the IDs of the created webhooks.

patch.description:
Update the configuration of an existing webhook, such as its URL,
enabled state, or event types.

200.description: Webhook updated successfully. Returns empty body.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a webhook. Any pending deliveries in the queue
for this webhook will also be removed.

delete.summary: Empty a document's queue of undelivered payloads
delete.description:
Clear all pending webhook deliveries for this document. Use this
if the queue has built up due to unreachable endpoints.

200.description: Queue cleared successfully. Returns empty body.

delete.summary: Clear queue for a specific webhook
delete.description:
Clear the queue of pending payloads for a specific webhook.
Only document owners can call this endpoint.

get.summary: Run an SQL query against a document
get.description:
Execute a read-only SQL SELECT query against the document's SQLite database.
This is a simplified endpoint for basic queries. For queries with parameters
or custom timeouts, use the POST endpoint instead.

schema.description: The SQL query to run. This GET endpoint is a simplified version of the corresponding POST endpoint, without support for parameters or options. See the POST endpoint for details of what's allowed in the SQL query string.

200.description: The result set for the query.

post.summary: Run an SQL query against a document, with options or parameters
post.description:
Execute a read-only SQL SELECT query with support for parameterized queries
and custom timeouts. All Grist documents are SQLite databases, and queries
are executed directly against SQLite with security restrictions.

sql.description: The SQL query to run. Must be a single SELECT statement, with no trailing semicolon. WITH clauses are permitted. All Grist documents are currently SQLite databases, and the SQL query is interpreted and run by SQLite, with various defensive measures. Statements that would modify the database are not supported.

timeout.description: Timeout after which operations on the document will be interrupted. Specified in milliseconds. Defaults to 1000 (1 second). This default is controlled by an optional environment variable read by the Grist app, GRIST_SQL_TIMEOUT_MSEC. The default cannot be exceeded, only reduced.

200.description: The result set for the query.

get.description: Returns the profile information of the currently authenticated user.

post.description: Update the display name for the current user.
Update the locale preference for the current user.

locale.description: Locale code (e.g. 'en-US', 'fr'). Set to null to clear.

get.description: Returns the current user's API key if one exists.

post.description: Create a new API key or regenerate an existing one.

force.description: If true, regenerate even if a key already exists

get.description: Returns information about the active user and organization for the current session.

orgError.description: Error information if org access failed

get.summary: Get all session users and organizations
get.description:
Returns all user profiles logged into the current session and all

delete.description:
This action also deletes the user's personal organisation and all the workspaces and documents it contains.
Currently, only the users themselves are allowed to delete their own accounts.

⚠️ **This action cannot be undone, please be cautious when using this endpoint** ⚠️

name.description: The user's name to delete (for confirmation, to avoid deleting the wrong account).

200.description: The account has been deleted successfully

400.description: The passed user name does not match the one retrieved from the database given the passed user id

403.description: The caller is not allowed to delete this account

post.description:
A disabled user can not log in and loses access to all pages,
including public ones, as well as API access. The operation is
non-destructive. Disabled users can be re-enabled.

Only admin accounts can disable a user.

200.description: The account has been disabled successfully

403.description: The caller is not allowed to disable this account

post.description:
If a user has been disabled, this will restore their access, including API access.

Only admin accounts can enable a user.

200.description: The account has been enabled successfully

403.description: The caller is not allowed to enable this account

get.description: This lists all your service accounts with their details.

delete.description: This deletes a given service account.

post.summary: Generate a new service account's api key

This renews an api key of a given service account.
Useful especially to change the API key when it has been leaked.

200.description: The service account with the new API key

403.description: The caller is not allowed to regenerate this api key

delete.summary: Delete a service account's api key
delete.description:
Deletes the API key for a service account. The service account will
no longer be able to authenticate until a new key is generated.

200.description: API key deleted successfully. Returns empty body.

403.description: The caller is not allowed to delete this api key

get.description:
Roles are system-defined entities giving particular permissions to a given resource (document, workspace or organization).
This API returns by default all these roles associated to all the resources.
You may use the parameters to filter the results.
Retrieve role by ID

put.description:
⚠️ This operation overwrites all the memberships (the "members" property). In order to update part of the membership, please use [PATCH](#tag/scim/operation/patchRoleById) instead.
Only the memberships are authorized to be modified through this endpoint, other properties will be ignored.

patch.summary: Partially update a role by ID
patch.description: Only the memberships are authorized to be modified through this endpoint, other properties will be ignored.

requestBody.description: Data for the partial update of the role.

tags.name: orgs
tags.description: Team sites and personal spaces are called 'orgs' in the API.

tags.name: workspaces
tags.description: Sites can be organized into groups of documents called workspaces.

tags.name: docs
tags.description: Workspaces contain collections of Grist documents.

tags.name: records
tags.description: Tables contain collections of records (also called rows).

tags.name: tables
tags.description: Documents are structured as a collection of tables.

tags.name: columns
tags.description: Tables are structured as a collection of columns.

tags.name: data
tags.description: Work with table data, using a (now deprecated) columnar format. We now recommend the `records` endpoints.

tags.name: attachments
tags.description: Documents may include attached files. Data records can refer to these using a column of type `Attachments`.

tags.name: webhooks
tags.description: Document changes can trigger requests to URLs called webhooks.

tags.name: sql
tags.description: Sql endpoint to query data from documents.

tags.name: service accounts
tags.description: Impersonations to manage grist resources through REST APIs with specific rights.

ApiKey.description: Access to the Grist API is controlled by an Authorization header, which should contain the word 'Bearer', followed by a space, followed by your API key.

DocComparison.description: Comparison result between two document versions

summary.description:
Relationship summary:
- same: documents have the same most recent state
- left: the left document has actions not yet in the right
- right: the right document has actions not yet in the left
- both: both documents have changes (possible divergence)
- unrelated: no common history found

leftChanges.description: Changes in the left document relative to common ancestor

rightChanges.description: Changes in the right document relative to common ancestor

memo.example: Send an email when a project is added

fields.description: A JSON object mapping column names to [cell values](https://support.getgrist.com/code/modules/GristData/#cellvalue).
A JSON object mapping column names to [cell values](https://support.getgrist.com/code/modules/GristData/#cellvalue).

require.description: keys are column identifiers, and values are [cell values](https://support.getgrist.com/code/modules/GristData/#cellvalue) we want to have in those columns (either by matching with an existing record, or creating a new record)

fields.description: keys are column identifiers, and values are [cell values](https://support.getgrist.com/code/modules/GristData/#cellvalue) to place in those columns (either overwriting values in an existing record, or in a new record)

tableRef.description: Row ID of the table in the _grist_Tables metadata table

onDemand.description: Whether the table uses on-demand loading

tableId.description: Set to a new value to rename the table

onDemand.description: Whether the table should use on-demand loading

colId.description: Set it to the new column ID when you want to change it.

type.description: Column type, by default Any. Ref, RefList and DateTime types requires a postfix, e.g. DateTime:America/New_York, Ref:Users

isFormula.description: Use "true" to indicate that the column is a formula column. Use "false" for trigger formula column.

widgetOptions.description: A JSON object with widget options, e.g.: {"choices": ["cat", "dog"], "alignment": "right"}

untieColIdFromLabel.description: Use "true" to indicate that the column label should not be used as the column identifier. Use "false" to use the label as the identifier.

recalcWhen.description:
A number indicating when the column should be recalculated.
  1. On new records or when any field in recalcDeps changes, it's a 'data-cleaning'.
  2. Never.
  3. Calculate on new records and on manual updates to any data field.

visibleCol.description: For Ref and RefList columns, the colRef of a column to display

recalcDeps.description: An encoded array of column identifiers (colRefs) that this column depends on. If any of these columns change, the column will be recalculated. E.g.: [2, 3]
An array of column identifiers (colRefs) that this column depends on, prefixed with "L" constant. If any of these columns change, the column will be recalculated. E.g.: ["L", 2, 3]

statement.description: A copy of the SQL statement.

name.description: The ID (technical name) of the table

title.description: The human readable name of the table

path.description: The URL to download the CSV

DocumentAttachmentsLocation.description: The current location of attachment files in the document

csvddfVersion.title: CSV Dialect schema version
csvddfVersion.description: A number to indicate the schema version of CSV Dialect. Version 1.0 was named CSV Dialect Description Format and used different field names.

delimiter.title: Delimiter
delimiter.description: A character sequence to use as the field separator.

doubleQuote.title: Double Quote
doubleQuote.description: Specifies the handling of quotes inside fields.
doubleQuote.context: If Double Quote is set to true, two consecutive quotes must be interpreted as one.

lineTerminator.title: Line Terminator
lineTerminator.description: Specifies the character sequence that must be used to terminate rows.

nullSequence.title: Null Sequence
nullSequence.description: Specifies the null sequence, for example, `\N`.

quoteChar.title: Quote Character
quoteChar.description: Specifies a one-character string to use as the quoting character.

escapeChar.title: Escape Character
escapeChar.description: Specifies a one-character string to use as the escape character.

skipInitialSpace.title: Skip Initial Space
skipInitialSpace.description: Specifies the interpretation of whitespace immediately following a delimiter. If false, whitespace immediately after a delimiter should be treated as part of the subsequent field.

header.title: Header
header.description: Specifies if the file includes a header row, always as the first row in the file.

commentChar.title: Comment Character
commentChar.description: Specifies that any row beginning with this one-character string, without preceeding whitespace, causes the entire line to be ignored.

caseSensitiveHeader.title: Case Sensitive Header
caseSensitiveHeader.description: Specifies if the case of headers is meaningful.
caseSensitiveHeader.context: Use of case in source CSV files is not always an intentional decision. For example, should "CAT" and "Cat" be considered to have the same meaning.

table-schema.title: Table Schema
table-schema.description: A Table Schema for this resource, compliant with the [Table Schema](/tableschema/) specification.

oneOf.title: String Field
oneOf.description: The field contains strings, that is, sequences of characters.

description.title: Description
description.description: A text description. Markdown is encouraged.

example.title: Example
example.description: An example value for the field.

type.description: The type keyword, which `MUST` be a value of `string`.

format.description: The format keyword options for `string` are `default`, `email`, `uri`, `binary`, and `uuid`.
format.context:
The following `format` options are supported:
  * **default**: any valid string.
  * **email**: A valid email address.
  * **uri**: A valid URI.
  * **binary**: A base64 encoded string representing binary data.
  * **uuid**: A string that is a uuid.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.description: The following constraints are supported for `string` fields.

required.description: Indicates whether a property must have a value for each instance.
required.context: An empty string is considered to be a missing value.

unique.description: When `true`, each value for the property `MUST` be unique.

pattern.description: A regular expression pattern to test each value of the property against, where a truthy response indicates validity.
pattern.context: Regular expressions `SHOULD` conform to the [XML Schema regular expression syntax](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#regexs).

minLength.description: An integer that specifies the minimum length of a value.

maxLength.description: An integer that specifies the maximum length of a value.

rdfType.description: The RDF type for this field.

oneOf.title: Number Field
oneOf.description: The field contains numbers of any kind including decimals.
oneOf.context:
The lexical formatting follows that of decimal in [XMLSchema](https://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#decimal): a non-empty finite-length sequence of decimal digits separated by a period as a decimal indicator. An optional leading sign is allowed. If the sign is omitted, '+' is assumed. Leading and trailing zeroes are optional. If the fractional part is zero, the period and following zero(es) can be omitted. For example: '-1.23', '12678967.543233', '+100000.00', '210'.

The following special string values are permitted (case does not need to be respected):
  - NaN: not a number
  - INF: positive infinity
  - -INF: negative infinity

A number `MAY` also have a trailing:
  - exponent: this `MUST` consist of an E followed by an optional + or - sign followed by one or more decimal digits (0-9)
  - percentage: the percentage sign: `%`. In conversion percentages should be divided by 100.

If both exponent and percentages are present the percentage `MUST` follow the exponent e.g. '53E10%' (equals 5.3).

description.title: Description
description.description: A text description. Markdown is encouraged.

example.title: Example
example.description: An example value for the field.

type.description: The type keyword, which `MUST` be a value of `number`.

format.description: There are no format keyword options for `number`: only `default` is allowed.

bareNumber.title: bareNumber
bareNumber.description: a boolean field with a default of `true`. If `true` the physical contents of this field must follow the formatting constraints already set out. If `false` the contents of this field may contain leading and/or trailing non-numeric characters (which implementors MUST therefore strip). The purpose of `bareNumber` is to allow publishers to publish numeric data that contains trailing characters such as percentages e.g. `95%` or leading characters such as currencies e.g. `€95` or `EUR 95`. Note that it is entirely up to implementors what, if anything, they do with stripped text.

decimalChar.description: A string whose value is used to represent a decimal point within the number. The default value is `.`.

groupChar.description: A string whose value is used to group digits within the number. The default value is `null`. A common value is `,` e.g. '100,000'.

required.context: An empty string is considered to be a missing value.

unique.description: When `true`, each value for the property `MUST` be unique.

format.description: The format keyword options for `date` are `default`, `any`, and `{PATTERN}`.
format.context:
The following `format` options are supported:
  * **default**: An ISO8601 format string of YYYY-MM-DD.
  * **any**: Any parsable representation of a date. The implementing library can attempt to parse the datetime via a range of strategies.
  * **{PATTERN}**: The value can be parsed according to `{PATTERN}`, which `MUST` follow the date formatting syntax of C / Python [strftime](http://strftime.org/).

oneOf.title: GeoPoint Field
oneOf.description: The field contains data describing a geographic point.

description.title: Description
description.description: A text description. Markdown is encouraged.

example.title: Example
example.description: An example value for the field.

type.description: The type keyword, which `MUST` be a value of `geopoint`.

format.description: The format keyword options for `geopoint` are `default`,`array`, and `object`.
format.context:
The following `format` options are supported:
  * **default**: A string of the pattern 'lon, lat', where `lon` is the longitude and `lat` is the latitude.
  * **array**: An array of exactly two items, where each item is either a number, or a string parsable as a number, and the first item is `lon` and the second item is `lat`.
  * **object**: A JSON object with exactly two keys, `lat` and `lon`
notes: Implementations `MUST` strip all white space in the default format of `lon, lat`.

oneOf.title: Duration Field
oneOf.description: The field contains a duration of time.
oneOf.context: The lexical representation for duration is the [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations) extended format `PnYnMnDTnHnMnS`, where `nY` represents the number of years, `nM` the number of months, `nD` the number of days, 'T' is the date/time separator, `nH` the number of hours, `nM` the number of minutes and `nS` the number of seconds. The number of seconds can include decimal digits to arbitrary precision. Date and time elements including their designator may be omitted if their value is zero, and lower order elements may also be omitted for reduced precision. Here we follow the definition of [XML Schema duration datatype](http://www.w3.org/TR/xmlschema-2/#duration) directly and that definition is implicitly inlined here.

fields.description: An `array` of Table Schema Field objects.

primaryKey.description: A primary key is a field name or an array of field names, whose values `MUST` uniquely identify each row in the table.
primaryKey.context: Field name in the `primaryKey` `MUST` be unique, and `MUST` match a field name in the associated table. It is acceptable to have an array with a single value, indicating that the value of a single field is the primary key.

items.description: Fields that make up the primary key.

fields.description: Fields that make up the primary key.

missingValues.description: Values that when encountered in the source, should be considered as `null`, 'not present', or 'blank' values.
missingValues.context:
Many datasets arrive with missing data values, either because a value was not collected or it never existed.
Missing values may be indicated simply by the value being empty in other cases a special value may have been used e.g. `-`, `NaN`, `0`, `-9999` etc.
The `missingValues` property provides a way to indicate that these values should be interpreted as equivalent to null.

`missingValues` are strings rather than being the data type of the particular field. This allows for comparison prior to casting and for fields to have missing value which are not of their type, for example a `number` field to have missing values indicated by `-`.

The default value of `missingValue` for a non-string type field is the empty string `''`. For string type fields there is no default for `missingValue` (for string fields the empty string `''` is a valid value and need not indicate null).

primary.description: Whether this is the primary email.

displayName.description: the display name of the user

preferredLanguage.description: Indicates the user's preferred written or spoken languages and is generally used for selecting a localized user interface.

locale.description: Used to indicate the User's default location for purposes of localizing such items as currency, date time format, or numerical representations.

photos.description: The picture of the user. It is expected to have a single item in the context of Grist.

primary.description: Whether this is the primary picture

type.description: The picture type. Currently, we only offer pictures of type "photo".

id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

itemsPerPage.description: Number of users returned per page.

attributes.description: A multi-valued list of strings indicating the names of resource attributes to return in the response.

excludedAttributes.description: A multi-valued list of strings indicating the names of resource attributes to be removed from the default set of attributes to return.

count.description: The number of resources to retrieve.

display.description: The display name of a member.

type.description: The type of member (User or Group).

$ref.description: The URL to access the member

id.description: The unique identifier of the group.

itemsPerPage.description: Number of groups returned per page.

displayName.description: The display name of the group.

display.description: The display name of a member.

type.description: The type of member (User or Group).

$ref.description: The URL to access the member

id.description: The unique identifier of the role.

orgId.description: The ID of the organization that the role applies to

workspaceId.description: The ID of the workspace that the role applies to

docId.description: The ID of the document that the role applies to

itemsPerPage.description: Number of roles returned per page.

filterQueryParam.name: filter
filterQueryParam.description: This is a JSON object mapping column names to arrays of allowed values.  For example, to filter column `pet` for values `cat` and `dog`, the filter would be `{"pet": ["cat", "dog"]}`. JSON contains characters that are not safe to place in a URL, so it is important to url-encode them.  For this example, the url-encoding is `%7B%22pet%22%3A%20%5B%22cat%22%2C%20%22dog%22%5D%7D`. See https://rosettacode.org/wiki/URL_encoding for how to url-encode a string, or https://www.urlencoder.org/ to try some examples. Multiple columns can be filtered. For example the filter for `pet` being either `cat` or `dog`, AND `size` being either `tiny` or `outrageously small`, would be `{"pet": ["cat", "dog"], "size": ["tiny", "outrageously small"]}`.

sortQueryParam.name: sort
sortQueryParam.description: Order in which to return results. If a single column name is given (e.g. `pet`), results are placed in ascending order of values in that column. To get results in an order that was previously prepared manually in Grist, use the special `manualSort` column name. Multiple columns can be specified, separated by commas (e.g. `pet,age`). For descending order, prefix a column name with a `-` character (e.g. `pet,-age`). To include additional sorting options append them after a colon (e.g. `pet,-age:naturalSort;emptyLast,owner`). Available options are: `orderByChoice`, `naturalSort`, `emptyLast`. Without the `sort` parameter, the order of results is unspecified.

limitQueryParam.name: limit
limitQueryParam.description: Return at most this number of rows.  A value of 0 is equivalent to having no limit.

colIdPathParam.name: colId
colIdPathParam.description: The column id (without the starting `$`) as shown in the column configuration below the label

tableIdPathParam.name: tableId
tableIdPathParam.description: normalized table name (see `TABLE ID` in Raw Data) or numeric row ID in `_grist_Tables`

orgIdPathParam.name: orgId
orgIdPathParam.description: This can be an integer id, or a string subdomain (e.g. `gristlabs`), or `current` if the org is implied by the domain in the url

noparseQueryParam.name: noparse
noparseQueryParam.description: Set to true to prohibit parsing strings according to the column type.

hiddenQueryParam.name: hidden
hiddenQueryParam.description: Set to true to include the hidden columns (like "manualSort")

headerQueryParam.name: header
headerQueryParam.description: Format for headers. Labels tend to be more human-friendly while colIds are more normalized.

searchCount.name: count
searchCount.description: The number of resources to retrieve.

searchFilter.name: filter
searchFilter.description: Filter resources based on specific criteria.
